name: Ordering Coffee
description: Cha chaan teng, milk tea, iced drinks

phrases.romanization: Ngo⁵ seung² jiu³ jat¹ bui¹ naai⁵ caa⁴
phrases.jyutping: ngo5 seung2 jiu3 jat1 bui1 naai5 caa4
phrases.context: Ordering at a cha chaan teng
Specifying your drink preferences — this is a very common order
Ordering a hot milk tea — preferred on rainy days
A classic HK drink — lemon tea over ice
A uniquely HK drink — half coffee, half milk tea. Always order this here.
Literally 'plain coffee' — no milk, no sugar
Reduce the sugar in your drink — say this with any order
走 in food orders means 'remove' — 走甜 = remove the sweetness
Ask for less ice in your cold drink
Remove ice completely from a cold drink
The iconic HK bun — no pineapple inside, named for its crusty top
A HK classic — order fresh from the oven at any cha chaan teng
Deep-fried French toast with butter and syrup — a HK cha chaan teng staple
Tell them you're eating in, not taking away
Tell them you want it to go
Ask for the bill — say this loudly across the room, it's normal

phrases.romanization: Ho² m⁴ ho² ji³ jung⁶ baat³ daat⁶ tung¹?
phrases.jyutping: ho2 m4 ho2 ji3 jung6 baat3 daat6 tung1
phrases.english: Can I use my Octopus card?
phrases.context: HK's tap-and-go payment card — accepted nearly everywhere

words.english: can I / is it possible to

phrases.context: Check before ordering if payment by card is accepted
The most common question you'll hear at any HK drinks counter

phrases.english: Would you like something to eat?
phrases.context: Staff will ask this after you order a drink

words.english: do you want / would you like

phrases.context: Staff will ask this for every order

phrases.english: How much is that in total?
phrases.context: Ask the total before you pay

phrases.english: I'd like to change my order
phrases.context: Politely ask to modify what you just ordered

phrases.romanization: Dung³ naai⁵ caa⁴ goi² zou⁶ jit⁶
phrases.jyutping: dung3 naai5 caa4 goi2 zou6 jit6
phrases.english: Change the iced milk tea to hot
phrases.context: Swap your iced order for hot
Staff say this when they need a moment — or you can say it too
A classic HK drink — hot malted milk. Very popular at cha chaan tengs.
Hot chocolate at a cha chaan teng — richer than you'd expect
Order another drink — common for refills of hot drinks
Stop someone from giving you more food or filling your cup
Tell staff the food is delicious — they genuinely love to hear this
Ask for your drink to be put in a takeaway cup

phrases.romanization: M⁴ goi¹, bong¹ ngo⁵ daa² baau¹
phrases.jyutping: m4 goi1, bong1 ngo5 daa2 baau1
phrases.english: Could you pack this up for me?
phrases.context: Ask to have leftover food packed to take home

phrases.romanization: M⁴ goi¹, do¹ jat¹ seung⁶ faai³ ji⁴
phrases.jyutping: m4 goi1, do1 jat1 seung6 faai3 ji4
phrases.english: Excuse me, another pair of chopsticks please
phrases.context: Ask for extra chopsticks for sharing dishes
Ask to change an item in a set meal

phrases.english: Excuse me, can I get some sugar?
phrases.context: Ask for sugar on the side

name: At a Restaurant
description: Full restaurant experience from arrival to leaving

phrases.context: Staff will ask this the moment you walk in
Tell them how many are in your group
Ask if there's space, especially at busy restaurants
Ask the wait time before deciding to stay or go
Ask for a physical menu — many places in HK use QR codes now
Start your order with this — follow with the dish name
Ask staff what they recommend — they always have an opinion
Point at something on the menu you don't recognise
Tell them your dietary restriction — swap 牛 for 豬 (pork), 雞 (chicken) etc.
Tell them you eat vegetarian — be prepared for follow-up questions in HK
Swap 蝦 for your allergen: 花生 (peanuts), 奶 (dairy), 麩質 (gluten)
Ask for a refill of water or tea
Ask for extra rice — usually free or very cheap
Tell staff something is too hot for you
Compliment the food — this always gets a warm response
Catch a staff member's eye and say this — no need to wave
Ask to pay separately
Confirm the total before paying
Check before you sit down at smaller restaurants
Politely flag a wrong order
Tell the waiter there's been a mix-up with your order
Ask to return something and get the right dish

words.english: can I / is it possible to

phrases.english: Could I get a new one please
phrases.context: Ask for a replacement — clean glass, new bowl, etc.
Alert staff if food seems undercooked — important for safety

phrases.romanization: M⁴ goi¹ bong¹ ngo⁵ daa² baau¹
phrases.jyutping: m4 goi1 bong1 ngo5 daa2 baau1
phrases.english: Could you pack this up for me?
phrases.context: Ask for a doggy bag for leftovers
Staff will say this when your table is ready

phrases.romanization: Dang² sap⁶ fan¹ jung¹, dak¹ m⁴ dak¹?
phrases.jyutping: dang2 sap6 fan1 jung1, dak1 m4 dak1
phrases.english: Wait ten minutes, is that okay?
phrases.context: Staff will say this when there's a short wait for a table

phrases.jyutping: m4 goi1 do1 jat1 deoi3 faai3 ji4

phrases.context: Ask for extra chopsticks when sharing dishes

dialogueScenes.description: You ask about ingredients before ordering

name: Wet Market
description: Buying fresh produce at a HK wet market

phrases.english: Excuse me! (to get a vendor's attention)
phrases.context: Call out 老闆 to address any market vendor — it's respectful and expected

phrases.romanization: Ni¹ go³ gei² do¹ cin² jat¹ gan¹?
phrases.jyutping: ni1 go3 gei2 do1 cin2 jat1 gan1
phrases.english: How much per catty for this?
phrases.context: A catty (斤) is 600g in HK — the standard unit at wet markets
Order by the catty — change the number to suit: 兩斤 (two catties)
Order half a catty — useful for expensive items or small households
Always worth asking — the vendor will tell you proudly if it's just arrived

phrases.romanization: Gam¹ ziu¹ faan¹ ge³, hou² san¹ sin¹
phrases.jyutping: gam1 ziu1 faan1 ge3, hou2 san1 sin1
phrases.english: Came in this morning, very fresh
phrases.context: Vendors say this all the time — listen for it
Point and say this — useful when you can't name what you're pointing at
Point at something further away
Ask for a larger piece or item
Ask for a smaller piece

phrases.english: Can you go a bit cheaper?
phrases.context: Negotiate at market stalls — especially effective when buying larger quantities
The general word for fruit at markets
The general word for vegetables
Fresh fish is sold live at many HK wet markets
The general word for meat — 豬肉 (pork), 雞肉 (chicken), 牛肉 (beef)
HK has incredible fresh seafood at wet markets
Vendors will ask this — say 夠喇 if you're done
Tell the vendor you're done — also works when they're piling up your bag

phrases.english: Could you pick out nice ones for me?
phrases.context: Ask the vendor to handpick good quality items for you

words.english: pick out the nice / good ones

phrases.english: Can you debone it for me?
phrases.context: Ask the fishmonger or butcher to remove the bones
Ask the total when buying multiple items

phrases.english: Excuse me, could you... (to a young woman)
phrases.context: A polite way to address and get help from a young female vendor

phrases.context: The first thing staff ask at any dim sum restaurant
The most popular dim sum tea — dark, earthy, great with rich food
Fragrant jasmine tea — lighter and more floral than pu'er
Tap the table twice with two fingers to silently signal for a refill — very HK
The gold standard dim sum — how you judge a dim sum restaurant
Open-topped dumplings — pork and prawn, often topped with orange roe
Fluffy steamed buns filled with sweet BBQ pork — a dim sum classic
Silky rice noodle rolls filled with shrimp, beef, or char siu
Pan-fried cake made with daikon radish — crispy outside, soft inside
Dumplings come in bamboo steamers — order by the basket
Order another round of your favourite — you'll definitely want to
Stop ordering — you're full. Say this when the trolley lady comes around
Say this when the trolley comes past with something you want
Ask about an unfamiliar dish on the trolley
Steamed chicken feet in black bean sauce — a dim sum must-try
Ask for the bill — at dim sum they count your empty steamers to add it up
Tell staff you're finished so they can bring the bill
Dim sum staff love hearing this — say it with genuine enthusiasm

dialogueScenes.title: Ordering dim sum
dialogueScenes.description: Sunday dim sum — the full experience

turns.speaker: other
turns.english: What dim sum would you like?

turns.speaker: other

turns.romanization: Jiu³ m⁴ jiu³ caa¹ siu¹ baau¹?
turns.english: Would you like char siu bao?

name: At a Coffee Shop
description: Specialty coffee, milk alternatives, and modern café ordering

phrases.context: Order a latte — the most popular specialty coffee order in HK cafés

phrases.romanization: Jat¹ bui¹ kaa¹ bu³ kei⁴ nuo⁴
phrases.jyutping: jat1 bui1 kaa1 bu3 kei4 nuo4
phrases.context: Order a cappuccino — lighter and foamier than a latte
Order an Americano — espresso with hot water, the classic black coffee option
The most popular café order in HK — cold Americano over ice
Cold brew coffee — slow-steeped overnight, smooth and strong
A shot of espresso over ice — intense and quick
The most popular milk alternative in HK cafés — ask to swap your milk
Soy milk — widely available at HK cafés and cha chaan tengs
Almond milk — less common than oat but available at specialty cafés

phrases.romanization: Ho² m⁴ ho² ji³ wun⁶ jin³ mak⁶ naai⁵?
phrases.jyutping: ho2 m4 ho2 ji3 wun6 jin3 mak6 naai5
phrases.english: Can I swap to oat milk?
phrases.context: Ask to substitute regular milk with oat milk — usually adds $7-10
The size question — most HK cafés use small/large rather than Italian sizes
Order the larger size
Reduce the syrup in your drink — relevant for lattes with flavoured syrup
Remove all syrup or sugar from your drink
Ask for your coffee to go — 走杯 is the universal HK term for a to-go cup

phrases.romanization: Jau⁵ mou⁵ zi⁵ ji⁴ ngaa⁴ kaa¹ fe¹?
phrases.jyutping: jau5 mou5 zi5 ji4 ngaa4 kaa1 fe1
phrases.context: Ask at specialty cafés whether they roast their own beans — a mark of quality

phrases.romanization: Ni¹ go³ jyut⁶ faan³ hai⁶ bin¹ dou⁶ lei⁴ ge³?
phrases.jyutping: ni1 go3 jyut6 faan3 hai6 bin1 dou6 lei4 ge3
phrases.english: Where is this month's coffee from?
phrases.context: Ask about the origin of the single-origin coffee — specialty cafés love this question

phrases.english: Would you like anything to eat?
phrases.context: What the barista asks after you order — say 唔使喇多謝 to decline

words.english: would you like / do you want
